timeline.label: Filed $60M at-the-market equity distribution agreement; optional dilution overhang at rich valuation
Q3 FY2026 revenue $10.3M (down YoY); nine-month net income $8.5M; cash $36.9M
FY2025 trough: revenue $59.0M, net loss $3.9M, operating cash outflow $7.4M after SiC order pause
FY2024 cycle peak net income $33.2M on $66.2M revenue; SiC burn-in demand elevated
SiC wafer-level burn-in ramp drove revenue to $65.0M from $20.3M two years prior
Pre-boom base revenue $20.3M before SiC capacity build-out cycle
Annual meeting approved 2.5M-share equity plan increase and ESPP expansion

industry.structure: Semiconductor burn-in and test is a niche within wafer-fabrication equipment; Aehr competes with custom in-house test and broader ATE vendors on FOX-XP and WaferPak systems. Gross margins near 40% at scale but orders are lumpy.
industry.share_shift: Aehr gained share during the 2023–2024 SiC burn-in upcycle but FY2025 revenue fell 11% as a major customer paused capacity adds.
industry.trend: SiC power-device and AI accelerator burn-in remain structural growth narratives, while customer concentration and order timing drive earnings volatility. Deferred revenue and install-base upgrades partially offset system shipment gaps.
